auto_pause_delay_in_minutes.description: Specifies the delay in minutes before automatically pausing the database when no activity is detected; must be between 60 and 1,440.

collation.description: Specifies the database collation, which determines the rules for how data is sorted and compared. Must match supported SQL Server collations.

create_mode.description: Specifies the creation mode of the SQL database, such as 'Default', 'Copy', 'Secondary', or 'PointInTimeRestore'.

creation_source_database_id.description: Specifies the ID of the source database for database creation, required for creating a database from an existing database or a restore point.

elastic_pool_id.description: Specifies the ID of the Elastic Pool to associate the database with. Required if the database is part of an Elastic Pool.

enclave_type.description: Specifies the type of enclave to enable for the SQL database. Must be either "None" or "Virtualized".

geo_backup_enabled.description: Enables or disables geo-redundant backups for the database. Must be set to true or false.

identity.description: Specifies the managed identity to associate with the SQL database for authentication purposes.

import.description: Specifies whether the database should be created by importing a BACPAC file. Requires the source URI and storage account credentials.
import.type: list(object({administrator_login: string,administrator_login_password: string,authentication_type: string,storage_account_id: optional(string),storage_key: string,storage_key_type: string,storage_uri: string}))

ledger_enabled.description: Specifies whether the database should have ledger functionality enabled, ensuring tamper-evidence for transactions. Must be set to `true` for ledger-enabled databases.

license_type.description: Specifies the licensing model for the SQL database, either 'LicenseIncluded' or 'BasePrice'.

long_term_retention_policy.description: Defines the retention policy for long-term backups, specifying the duration and storage location for retaining database backups.
long_term_retention_policy.type: list(object({immutable_backups_enabled: optional(bool),monthly_retention: optional(string),week_of_year: optional(number),weekly_retention: optional(string),yearly_retention: optional(string)}))

maintenance_configuration_name.description: Specifies the name of the maintenance configuration to associate with the SQL database. Must be an existing configuration in the same region.

max_size_gb.description: Defines the maximum storage size for the database in gigabytes. Must be within the limits of the selected service tier.

min_capacity.description: Defines the minimum compute capacity (in DTUs or vCores) for the database, ensuring baseline performance.

name.description: Specifies the name of the SQL database, must be unique within the SQL server and follow Azure naming conventions.

read_replica_count.description: Specifies the number of read replicas to be created for the database. Must be a non-negative integer.

read_scale.description: Enables or disables read-scale functionality for the database, allowing for read-only replicas; valid values are `Enabled` or `Disabled`.

recover_database_id.description: ID of the source database to recover from, required when creating a database using the recovery feature.

recovery_point_id.description: Specifies the unique identifier of the recovery point to restore the database from.

restore_dropped_database_id.description: Specifies the ID of the dropped database to restore. Must be used with the restore point in time parameter.

restore_long_term_retention_backup_id.description: Specifies the ID of the long-term retention backup to restore from. Must be a valid backup ID in the same region and subscription.

restore_point_in_time.description: Specifies the point in time (ISO8601 format) to restore the database to, applicable only for point-in-time restore operations.

sample_name.description: Specifies the name of the SQL database; must be unique within the SQL server and adhere to Azure naming conventions.

secondary_type.description: Specifies the replication type for the secondary database; valid options are "Geo", "Named", or "None".

server_id.description: Specifies the ID of the existing SQL server to host the database.

short_term_retention_policy.description: Specifies the retention period for short-term backups, measured in days. Must be between 7 and 35 days.

sku_name.description: "Specifies the SKU name for the SQL database, determining the performance level and pricing tier."

storage_account_type.description: Specifies the storage account type for the database, determining performance and cost characteristics. Possible values include 'Standard' or 'Premium'.

tags.description: Key-value pairs used to categorize and organize resources for management and billing purposes.

threat_detection_policy.description: Defines the threat detection policy for the SQL database, specifying security alert configurations such as email recipients, retention days, and enabled alert types.
threat_detection_policy.type: list(object({disabled_alerts: optional(list(string)),email_account_admins: optional(string),email_addresses: optional(list(string)),retention_days: optional(number),state: optional(string),storage_account_access_key: optional(string),storage_endpoint: optional(string)}))

timeouts.description: Defines the time durations for create, update, and delete operations, allowing customization of operation timeouts.

transparent_data_encryption_enabled.description: Determines whether transparent data encryption is enabled for the SQL database; must be either true or false.

transparent_data_encryption_key_automati.description: Enables automatic rotation of the transparent data encryption key; must be set to true or false.

transparent_data_encryption_key_vault_ke.description: Specifies the Azure Key Vault key ID to use for transparent data encryption, requiring the key to be in the same region as the database.

zone_redundant.description: Specifies whether the database should be zone-redundant. Requires the database to be in a supported region and service tier.
